 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-33xf","title":"P6.13: CI/CD Pipeline Configuration","description":"# P6.13: CI/CD Pipeline Configuration

## Goal
Configure comprehensive CI/CD pipelines that run all tests, security checks, and builds on every PR, ensuring code quality and preventing regressions.

## Why This Task is Critical

Without CI/CD:
- Regressions slip through
- Security vulnerabilities undetected
- Cross-platform issues missed
- Manual testing burden increases

## Pipeline Components

### 1. GitHub Actions Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
  RUST_LOG: debug

jobs:
  # Rust linting and formatting
  lint:
    name: Lint
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: rustfmt, clippy
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      
      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
      
      - name: Run clippy
        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

  # Rust unit tests
  test-rust:
    name: Rust Tests
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      
      - name: Run tests
        run: cargo test --all-features --verbose -- --nocapture
        env:
          RUST_LOG: debug
      
      - name: Run doc tests
        run: cargo test --doc

  # JavaScript tests
  test-js:
    name: JavaScript Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        working-directory: web
        run: npm ci
      
      - name: Run tests
        working-directory: web
        run: npm test -- --coverage
      
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: web/coverage/lcov.info

  # Browser E2E tests
  test-e2e:
    name: E2E Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      
      - name: Cache cargo
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      
      - name: Build release
        run: cargo build --release
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      
      - name: Install Playwright
        working-directory: web
        run: |
          npm ci
          npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit
      
      - name: Run E2E tests
        working-directory: web
        run: npm run test:e2e
        env:
          DEBUG: pw:api
      
      - name: Upload test artifacts
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: web/playwright-report/

  # Security audit
  security:
    name: Security Audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      
      - name: Install cargo-audit
        run: cargo install cargo-audit
      
      - name: Run cargo audit
        run: cargo audit
      
      - name: npm audit
        working-directory: web
        run: npm audit --audit-level=high

  # Crypto test vectors
  crypto-vectors:
    name: Crypto Test Vectors
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      
      - name: Run crypto vector tests
        run: cargo test --test crypto_vectors -- --nocapture
        env:
          RUST_LOG: debug

  # Build artifacts
  build:
    name: Build
    needs: [lint, test-rust, test-js]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
          - os: macos-latest
            target: x86_64-apple-darwin
          - os: macos-latest
            target: aarch64-apple-darwin
          - os: windows-latest
            target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}
      
      - name: Build release
        run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }}
      
      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: cass-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/cass*
```

### 2. Benchmarks Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/bench.yml
name: Benchmarks

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  benchmark:
    name: Performance Benchmarks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # For comparing with main
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench --bench crypto_perf --bench db_perf -- --save-baseline pr
      
      - name: Compare with main
        if: github.event_name == pull_request
        run: |
          git checkout main
          cargo bench --bench crypto_perf --bench db_perf -- --save-baseline main
          git checkout -
          cargo bench --bench crypto_perf --bench db_perf -- --baseline main --load-baseline pr
      
      - name: Check for regressions
        run: python scripts/check_bench_regression.py --threshold 10
```

### 3. Fuzzing Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/fuzz.yml
name: Fuzzing

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 2 * * 0"  # Weekly Sunday 2am
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  fuzz:
    name: Fuzz Testing
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        target: [archive_parse, password_derive, fts_query, jsonl_parse]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust nightly
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
      
      - name: Install cargo-fuzz
        run: cargo install cargo-fuzz
      
      - name: Run fuzzer
        run: cargo fuzz run ${{ matrix.target }} -- -max_total_time=3600
        continue-on-error: true
      
      - name: Upload crashes
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: crashes-${{ matrix.target }}
          path: fuzz/artifacts/${{ matrix.target }}/
```

### 4. Release Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yml
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"

jobs:
  release:
    name: Release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Build all targets
        uses: ./.github/workflows/ci.yml
      
      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          files: |
            target/*/release/cass*
          generate_release_notes: true
```

### Logging Configuration

```rust
// Ensure tests produce detailed logs
#[cfg(test)]
fn init_test_logging() {
    let _ = tracing_subscriber::fmt()
        .with_env_filter("debug")
        .with_test_writer()
        .try_init();
}
```

## Test Requirements

### Pipeline Validation Tests

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# scripts/validate_ci.sh

set -e

echo "=== Validating CI Pipeline ==="

echo "1. Checking workflow syntax..."
for f in .github/workflows/*.yml; do
    echo "  Validating $f"
    yq . "$f" > /dev/null || { echo "Invalid YAML: $f"; exit 1; }
done

echo "2. Running local CI simulation..."
cargo fmt --check
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --all-features

echo "3. Building web assets..."
cd web && npm ci && npm test && npm run build

echo "=== CI Validation Complete ==="
```

## Files to Create

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: Main CI workflow
- `.github/workflows/bench.yml`: Benchmark workflow
- `.github/workflows/fuzz.yml`: Fuzzing workflow
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`: Release workflow
- `scripts/validate_ci.sh`: Local validation script
- `scripts/check_bench_regression.py`: Benchmark regression checker

## Exit Criteria

- [ ] All workflows pass on clean main
- [ ] PR checks block on failures
- [ ] Cross-platform builds working
- [ ] Security audit runs automatically
- [ ] Crypto vectors verified on every PR
- [ ] Benchmarks track performance over time
- [ ] Fuzzing runs weekly
- [ ] Release automation working
- [ ] Comprehensive logging in all tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-07T03:36:16.120161771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:32:47.212867848Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:32:47.212693895Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-33xf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h0uc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T03:36:22.426113423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-35pi","title":"Add PhaseTracker and metrics to e2e_filters.rs","description":"## Priority 1: Add PhaseTracker to e2e_filters.rs

### Current State
tests/e2e_filters.rs HAS basic E2E logging (E2eLogger, E2eTestInfo) but LACKS PhaseTracker for granular phase tracking.

### Required Changes

1. **Add PhaseTracker import:**
```rust
use util::e2e_log::{E2eError, E2eLogger, E2eTestInfo, PhaseTracker, E2ePerformanceMetrics};
```

2. **Wrap test functions with PhaseTracker:**
```rust
#[test]
fn test_filter_by_agent() {
    let tracker = PhaseTracker::new("e2e_filters", "test_filter_by_agent");
    
    let result = tracker.phase("setup_fixtures", "Creating test fixtures", || {
        setup_test_data()
    });
    
    tracker.phase("run_index", "Running indexer", || {
        run_cass_index(&temp_dir)
    });
    
    tracker.phase("test_agent_filter", "Testing --agent filter", || {
        run_filter_test("--agent", "claude")
    });
    
    tracker.phase("verify_results", "Verifying filter results", || {
        assert_filtered_output(&output, expected)
    });
    
    tracker.complete();
}
```

3. **Add metrics for filter query performance:**
```rust
tracker.metrics("filter_query_agent", &E2ePerformanceMetrics {
    duration_ms: elapsed.as_millis() as u64,
    items_processed: Some(result_count as u64),
    ..Default::default()
});
```

### Suggested Phases per Test
- setup_fixtures
- run_index  
- test_{filter_type} (agent, since, until, days, workspace, combined)
- verify_results
- cleanup

### Files to Modify
- tests/e2e_filters.rs

### Testing Requirements (CRITICAL)
After implementation, verify:

1. **JSONL Output Validation:**
```bash
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test e2e_filters test_filter_by_agent -- --nocapture
# Verify JSONL contains:
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq -r 'select(.test.suite == "e2e_filters")' | head -20
# Must have: run_start, test_start, phase_start, phase_end, metrics, test_end, run_end
```

2. **Phase Markers Present:**
```bash
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "phase_end" and .phase.name | startswith("test_"))'
```

3. **Metrics Captured:**
```bash
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and .name | startswith("filter_"))'
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PhaseTracker wraps all test functions
- [ ] Each filter type has its own phase
- [ ] Metrics capture filter query duration and result count
- [ ] JSONL output validates against test-results/e2e/SCHEMA.md
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] No regression in test execution time (< 10% increase)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:19:36.186050385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:24:29.178697948Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-35pi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2xq0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:04:01.218970802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-395v","title":"[Feature] Verbose Debug Logging Mode","description":"## Feature: Verbose Debug Logging Mode

Implement `E2E_VERBOSE=1` environment variable that enables detailed step-by-step logging.

### What It Does
When enabled, tests emit detailed debug information:
- Every file operation logged
- Every command with full arguments
- Every assertion with context
- Every state transition

### Implementation
1. **Environment check** - Check `E2E_VERBOSE` at test start
2. **Conditional logging** - `if verbose { log_debug\!(...) }`
3. **Separate output file** - `test-results/e2e/verbose_*.log`
4. **No JSONL impact** - Verbose logs are text, not JSONL events

### Usage
```bash
E2E_VERBOSE=1 ./scripts/e2e/connector_stress.sh
# Creates: test-results/e2e/verbose_connector_stress.log
```

### Rust Implementation
```rust
fn verbose_log(msg: &str) {
    if std::env::var("E2E_VERBOSE").is_ok() {
        eprintln\!("[VERBOSE] {}", msg);
        // Also append to verbose log file
    }
}
```

### Shell Implementation
```bash
verbose_log() {
    if [ -n "${E2E_VERBOSE:-}" ]; then
        echo "[VERBOSE] $*" | tee -a "$VERBOSE_LOG"
    fi
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `E2E_VERBOSE=1` enables verbose mode
- [ ] Verbose logs go to separate file
- [ ] Does not affect JSONL event stream
- [ ] All E2E scripts support verbose mode
- [ ] Verbose output includes timestamps","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:59.502864770Z","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:10:04.233534331Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-395v","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ohe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:26:08.170154256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
